title: zephyr: implement generic SCHC fragmentation engine
description: Follow-up from project-LICHEN-9nrr.11 review. The generic SCHC module now exposes schc_fragmenter_config, schc_fragmenter_init(), and schc_fragmenter_next(), but schc_fragmenter_next() deliberately returns SCHC_ERR_NOT_SUPPORTED. Implement RFC 8724 fragmentation/reassembly behavior for the generic module, including No-ACK, ACK-Always, and ACK-on-Error mode handling as appropriate for LICHEN LoRa MTUs. Add unit tests covering tile sizing, FCN/window behavior, buffer limits, and error handling. Keep profile-specific rule IDs/config outside the generic engine.
notes: Implemented generic SCHC bounded tile fragmentation and ordered reassembly in lichen/subsys/schc. Fragmenter now emits RuleID plus W/FCN control byte and one tile per fragment, supports configurable/default W and FCN widths, validates MTU/output sizes, rolls windows, emits All-1 on the final tile, and returns SCHC_ERR_DONE after completion. Added reassembler API that validates RuleID, W/FCN, tile sizing, in-order offsets, buffer limits, and completes on All-1. Added generic ztests for fragment tile emission, window rollover, MTU/output rejection, fragmenter-to-reassembler round trip, wrong rule, out-of-order, truncated, empty, and oversized fragments. Tests run: west build qemu_x86 lichen/tests/schc_generic passed; generic SCHC QEMU run passed 11/11 and printed PROJECT EXECUTION SUCCESSFUL before timeout cleanup; standalone SCHC vector test 12/12 passed; west build qemu_x86 lichen/tests/link_crypto passed; link_crypto QEMU run passed 2/2 and printed PROJECT EXECUTION SUCCESSFUL before timeout cleanup; git diff --check passed. Review pass 1 finding: ACK/MIC/DTag semantics are not representable in the current API and ACK modes currently share the No-ACK fragment wire format; documented this and filed project-LICHEN-9nrr.32. Review pass 2 findings: no further issues.
close_reason: Completed bounded generic SCHC fragmentation/reassembly engine, tests, and two review passes
